quote: Rules and procedures convey the message that "I'm here to teach and you're here to learn." They give students the structure they need and also help them feel that the classroom is a safe and predictable place.
author: Robert J. Marzano
work: A Handbook for Classroom Management That Works

quote: Like many peers teaching compulsory subjects, I've learned my enjoyment of reading may not be teachable; we have responsibility to equip students to access knowledge.
author: Rebecca Birch
work: On Chasing the Rainbow of Intrinsic Motivation

quote: By meeting these needs in the classroom teachers can effectively maximise student engagement while preventing behaviour problems which often arise due to feelings of boredom, frustration and alienation.
author: Rob Plevin
work: The Active Learning Tool Kit

quote: Solving problems brings pleasure. When I say 'problem solving' here, I mean any cognitive work that succeeds; it might be understanding a difficult passage of prose, planning a garden, or sizing up an investment opportunity.
author: Lauren Brown
work: What Exercise Taught Me about Student Motivation

quote: If they do not feel welcome, how can we expect them to participate, engage, and achieve?
author: Douglas Fisher, Nancy Frey & Ian Pumpian
work: How to Create a Culture of Achievement in Your School and Classroom

quote: The experience of waiting is not determined only by how long people wait. It is shaped by what the wait feels like.
author: Patrick Dempsey
work: Students Aren't Engaged, Motivated, or Interested

quote: Learning how to manage student behavior in the classroom is one of the most challenging and important skills teachers need to master.
author: Jim Knight
work: Instructional Coaching: A Partnership Approach to Improving Instruction

quote: If you were not worried about belonging before the new Ofsted framework, you are now. The new inspection toolkit defines belong as one of three core lenses through which inspectors will evaluate a school.
author: David Didau
work: Language of Belonging

quote: Student motivation does not just happen; teachers need to make a conscious effort to build and sustain it. Uninspired lessons do not yield inspired learners.
author: Suzy Pepper Rollins
work: Learning in the Fast Lane

quote: A student who does not yet command this register cannot fully inhabit the intellectual and moral world the school aims to induct them into.
author: David Didau
work: The Psychology of Belonging

quote: The thing that drives the lesson into our children's hearts after they make a mistake is our empathy and sadness.
author: Foster Cline & Jim Fay
work: Parenting with Love and Logic

quote: Authoritarian control much more often incites violence than prevents it.
author: Peter Gray
work: Lord of the Flies A Harmful Distortion of Childrens Nature

quote: It is this moment, this pause between heartbeats when you decide to follow through or not, that separates the happiest, most effective teachers from the rest.
author: Michael Linsin
work: Smart Classroom Management

quote: Looking just at the second data column, 58% of 10-year-olds, 27% of 14-year-olds, and 16% of 17-year-olds cannot leave their own yard without adult accompaniment.
author: Peter Gray
work: New Data on the Chaining of Children and Teens

quote: If you don't give them fun, they will make their own. If you don't give them a sense of empowerment, they will assert themselves in their own way. And if you don't help them feel valued, they will opt out.
author: Rob Plevin
work: Take Control of the Noisy Class

quote: I focused most of my attention on kids who were very carefully climbing up the side of a boulder and sliding down a plastic slide that had been set up against it. I was impressed by their natural caution.
author: Gray, Peter
work: Woodland Creatures at Play

quote: There is no such thing as boring content. In the hands of a great teacher, the material students need to master is exciting, interesting, and inspiring, even if we sometimes momentarily doubt that we can make it so.
author: Doug Lemov
work: Teach Like a Champion 2.0

quote: The Science of Reading is not in Conflict with 'Fostering the Love of Reading' But... a bloated basal could be in conflict with both if teachers aren't given the space and supports to supplement.
author: Karshner, Melinda
work: For the Love of Reading

quote: By teaching students to organize their work clearly, you set them up to succeed not just in your class but in future classes.
author: Doug Lemov
work: Teach Like a Champion Field Guide 2.0

quote: Having friendship energy is a common teacher mistake that causes a cascading of classroom management issues.
author: Michael Linsin
work: Why You Should Never Ever Be Friends With Students

quote: Challenge is good and will increase concentration, improve memory, and keep your students focused and purpose-driven from the moment they walk through the door.
author: Michael Linsin
work: The Smart Classroom Management Way

quote: All students want to like their teacher. But most of them don't.
author: Michael Linsin
work: Why Your Students Don't Like You And What To Do About It

quote: Research shows that when visual stimuli are chaotic, children tend to behave chaotically. A clutter-filled room is distracting and stressful.
author: Michael Linsin
work: The Total Classroom Management Makeover

quote: Young people are not disengaging from learning. In many cases, they are disengaging from systems that no longer feel connected to their lives, identities, relationships, or futures.
author: Katie Martin
work: Why So Many Kids Are Checking Out of School

quote: Creating the climate and environment conducive for learning is imperative. Brains have to be safe in order to be fully engaged and to avoid reverting to fight, flight, or freeze mode.
author: Gayle Gregory & Martha Kaufeldt
work: The Motivated Brain

quote: Sure, you might be able to say all of the right words! 'Short cycle formative assessment!', 'full participation', 'non-volunteers', but in the case of leading explicit instruction, the devil is in the details.
author: Shaun (Mutally Assured Instruction)
work: The Room Where It Happens

quote: At any age, people need to pause and process what they're learning. They need to chew on concepts, jot down their thoughts, and compare understandings with peers.
author: Pérsida Himmele & William Himmele
work: Total Participation Techniques

quote: The lesson travels from Do Now to recap to new learning and checks for understanding on a constant short cycle gradually fading scaffolding towards independence.
author: Mutually Assured Instruction
work: EI? EI? Oh!

quote: Children need strong teachers who are friendly — not friendly teachers who are pushovers.
author: Jim Fay & Charles Fay
work: Teaching with Love and Logic

quote: Teachers of explicit instruction are relative professionals when it comes to short cycle formative assessment. Teaching as a profession is also obsessed with an acronym, potentially to our own detriment.
author: Mutually Assured Instruction
work: I.C.E. I.C.E. Baby

quote: A class is not a concrete unit but a room full of individuals who may have to be reached in different ways.
author: Vickie Gill
work: The Ten Students You'll Meet in Your Classroom

quote: In its simplest form, belonging is the feeling of being accepted and valued in a community. A place where you can be your unfiltered self without a fear of judgement or exclusion.
author: Mutually Assured Instruction
work: Longing for Belonging

quote: Great teachers establish clear expectations at the start of the year and follow them consistently as the year progresses.
author: Todd Whitaker
work: What Great Teachers Do Differently

quote: The big issue with proactive approaches is that if they're done well, you're unlikely to need a consultant long term to fix all of your problems because the problems will be minimal and actionable.
author: Mutually Assured Instruction
work: Pros and Con(men) Why Restorative Conversations Are a Reactive Approach to Managing Behaviour

quote: With any classroom problem there are two ways of approaching it: reward positive behaviour or punish inappropriate behaviour.
author: Rob Plevin
work: The Behaviour Toolkit

quote: The human brain is severely limited. Working memory of an average person can range between 3 and 7 novel items as stimuli before it becomes overwhelmed and cannot process any additional information.
author: Mutually Assured Instruction
work: Strat Chat Chunking

quote: The climate of a classroom refers to the type of environment students are exposed to when they are learning, and much like the air we breathe, it is ignored until it becomes foul.
author: Kay Burke
work: What to Do with the Kid Who...

quote: Circulation is an important aspect of so many elements of our lives.
author: Mutually Assured Instruction
work: Strat Chat: Circulation

quote: An effective classroom has patterns and routines in place that make interaction and movement easy to organize and accomplish.
author: Edmund T. Emmer & Carolyn M. Evertson
work: Classroom Management for Middle and High School Teachers

quote: If every teacher is setting their own routine, for a student it isn't that different to there being no routine.
author: Mutually Assured Instruction
work: Strategic Chat Routines

quote: Effective classroom managers are aware of the unique needs of individual students; although many teachers sense such needs instinctively, it helps to formally identify categories of high-need students.
author: Robert J. Marzano
work: Classroom Management That Works

quote: Over the past decade I have noticed a steady decline in the prior knowledge that my students come to school with.
author: Mutually Assured Instruction
work: The Cost of Giving Crisis

quote: Meeting students' innate psychological needs, particularly in terms of building positive relationships and acknowledging their efforts, is fundamental to gaining their trust and respect.
author: Rob Plevin
work: Classroom Management Success in 7 Days or Less

quote: No student can tell themselves 'I don't get it', 'this is hard', 'I'll just chill till the teacher notices me'. They can have no external excuses.
author: Dominic Salles
work: Get them to behave with no skills whatsoever: Part 1 - Narrate What You See

quote: Classroom teachers determine the learning environment in their classrooms; every action and inaction establishes it as ultimately positive, neutral, or negative.
author: J. Roth
work: Classroom Management for Successful Instruction

quote: The PPT is not the same as the work in front of the student. So, the PPT is usually abstract. It contains lots of words which they can't commit to memory before you move on to the next slide.
author: Dominic Salles
work: Get Them to Behave With No Skills Whatsoever Part 2 The Visualiser

quote: Students will always respond more positively to a teacher they know, like and trust.
author: Rob Plevin
work: Connect With Your Students

quote: Executive function is often described as the brain's air traffic control system, which is a useful image because childhood is basically a small airport where every plane wants to land at once.
author: Kevin Stinehart
work: Executive Function: The Architecture of Childhood

quote: The hidden power of language is its ability to subtly convey messages that shape our thinking, sense of self, and group affinity.
author: Ron Ritchhart
work: Creating Cultures of Thinking

quote: The look on that boy's face was different from the look children get when an adult solves the problem for them. It was not gratitude. It was ownership. That is agency. That is capability.
author: Kevin Stinehart
work: Independence and Agency The Architecture of Childhood

quote: It's important that we access and sustain optimism so that we can continually persist in making it difficult for our students to fail.
author: Richard Curwin
work: Discipline with Dignity: New Challenges, New Solutions

quote: Children become capable by repeatedly doing the kinds of things that require capability.
author: Kevin Stinehart
work: Kids Not Childhood

quote: Your role becomes one of support and clarification for students' ideas; students move from being passive listeners to active participants in the creation of meaning.
author: Laura E. Pinto
work: From Discipline to Culturally Responsive Engagement

quote: The Architecture of Childhood is not metaphorical. There is a sequence to it. Physical competence comes first -- not because it matters more, but because it builds the platform on which everything else rests.
author: Kevin Stinehart
work: Physical Health and Coordination: The Architecture of Childhood

quote: Respond rather than react. Don't waste your emotional oxygen huffing and puffing about your kids' behavior.
author: Karis Kimmel Murray
work: Grace-Based Discipline

quote: What if we are not under-instructing children? What if we are over-blasting them? What if some learning does not stick because the school day gives children almost no time for it to settle?
author: Kevin Stinehart
work: Recovery Is the New Rigor

quote: They're just kids in need of direction and recalibration, in need of someone to restore their dignity and switch on their intrinsic motivational engines.
author: Michael Linsin
work: Smart Classroom Management, Vol. 1

quote: Maybe they are fragile because childhood got too protected from the right kinds of hard.
author: Kevin Stinehart
work: Resilience & Grit The Architecture of Childhood

quote: It's not possible to influence and inspire students when you're just trying to keep your head above water.
author: Michael Linsin
work: Smart Classroom Management, Vol. 2

quote: In other words, it grows from the exact things modern childhood keeps pushing to the margins.
author: Kevin Stinehart
work: Self-Regulation The Architecture of Childhood

quote: Effective classroom management is about action. It's about doing something in response to misbehavior, not talking about it.
author: Michael Linsin
work: Smart Classroom Management, Vol. 3

quote: That is the second piece of my Architecture of Childhood [ ]: social connection. The skills, characteristics, and capacities (outputs) we want for children do not appear magically.
author: Stinehart, Kevin
work: Social Connection The Architecture of Childhood

quote: Front-load the means of participation to ensure students can pay attention to how you want them to respond.
author: Craig Barton
work: Tips for Teachers: 400+ Ideas to Improve Your Teaching

quote: The most dangerous thing schools do isn't holding gifted students back. It's making them doubt they're gifted.
author: Kevin Stinehart
work: The Most Dangerous Thing Schools Can Produce Is a Child Who No Longer Knows They Are Gifted

quote: Research indicates that rules and procedures should not simply be imposed on students; they should be created with students.
author: Robert J. Marzano
work: A Handbook for Classroom Management That Works

quote: Given space, kids do not self-destruct the way we fear they might. They self-organize.
author: Kevin Stinehart
work: We Opened the Playground Door and Accidentally Started a Statewide Movement

quote: If you don't give them fun, they'll make their own. If you don't give them a sense of power, they will assert themselves in their own way. And if you don't make them feel valued they will likely opt out.
author: Rob Plevin
work: The Active Learning Tool Kit

quote: We gave them a seven-hour school day with ever-shrinking recess, vanishing time to communicate and move, and an ever-growing list of difficult cognitive and behavioral demands. Then we wondered why they put the cart on the curb.
author: Stinehart, Kevin
work: We Removed the Shopping Cart Corrals

quote: When students are immersed in a welcoming culture, they in turn sustain and extend that welcome to others.
author: Douglas Fisher, Nancy Frey & Ian Pumpian
work: How to Create a Culture of Achievement in Your School and Classroom

quote: What we have the opportunity to do in school is so much bigger than just preparing to participate in the economy. We're preparing to participate in the world!
author: Dave Stuart Jr.
work: Mandy Gets It

quote: If students ignore their teachers' lessons, disrupt the flow of instruction, or act rudely, they inhibit the learning of every student in the classroom, including themselves.
author: Jim Knight
work: Instructional Coaching: A Partnership Approach to Improving Instruction

quote: This has become the class I most look forward to teaching every day -- they are willing to wrestle with big ideas, to be vulnerable, and to take risks.
author: Dave Stuart Jr.
work: A Collaborative Approach for Crafting Everest Statements With Your Students

quote: If you've attempted today to help your students grow in strength or knowledge or ability or wisdom, you are, in fact, not a bum.
author: Dave Stuart Jr.
work: You're Not a Bum

quote: Regret isn’t just a rumination. Used well, it’s a source of information. It tells us what we value, what we wish we had done differently, and, most usefully, what we want to do now.
author: Dave Stuart Jr.
source: Dave Stuart Jr. on Substack, December 16, 2025
work: Got Regrets Yet Teachers and Four Types of Regret

quote: As Kurt Lewin argued, behaviour is a function of both the person and their environment. Effective schools recognise that behaviour is shaped not only by individual choices but by the structures surrounding those choices.
author: Richard Wheadon
work: We Build Fences Because Children Run

quote: Predictable consequences strengthen behaviour. Inconsistent consequences create unstable behaviour patterns. Unpredictable reinforcement leads to confusion, resistance, and erratic responses.
author: Wheadon, Richard
work: What Skinner Criminology and a Year 9 Boy Taught Me About Behaviour

quote: There is power in service that no one will ever know you gave.
author: Stuart, Dave

quote: Intrinsic motivation has long been the holy grail; but it's a pipe dream for most subject teachers—communicating the value of knowledge is more fruitful.
author: Rebecca Birch
work: On Chasing the Rainbow of Intrinsic Motivation

quote: It's notable too that the pleasure is in the solving of the problem. Working on a problem with no sense that you're making progress is not pleasurable. In fact, it's frustrating.
author: Lauren Brown
work: What Exercise Taught Me about Student Motivation

quote: Higher education has spent a very long time defending discomfort while neglecting the conditions that help human beings endure it.
author: Patrick Dempsey
work: Students Aren't Engaged, Motivated, or Interested

quote: We learn not just from so-called "direct experience" with Nature, but also from the experience of others, who, like ourselves, have acquired knowledge as the result of the experience of previous generations.
author: Michael Baker, Jerry Andriessen & Sanna Järvelä
work: Affective Learning Together

quote: The individual in contemporary society is not so much described by tests as constructed by them (Allan Hanson, 1994)
author: 100% Assessment
work: Creating People

quote: Students learn more readily when they can connect what they are learning to what they already know. However, instructors should not assume that students will immediately or naturally draw on relevant prior knowledge.
author: Susan A. Ambrose et al.
work: How Learning Works

quote: The equity problem with contextualised assessment is not that disadvantaged students are less intelligent. It is that unfamiliar contexts impose a dual cognitive load: process the context and retrieve the concept simultaneously.
author: 100% Assessment
work: The Use of Context in Assessment

quote: Subitizing is a low-key subconscious operation, while counting provokes significant cerebral activity.
author: David A. Sousa
work: How the Brain Learns Mathematics

quote: The original letter clearly signaled the ways a student could opt out, but didn't clearly explain the benefits.
author: Chad Aldeman
work: Congratulations! Your Child Belongs in Algebra

quote: Emotions take precedence over all other brain processes; the agree/disagree statements you open a lesson with will evoke emotions in most students and help them understand the concepts being shared.
author: Marilee Sprenger
work: How to Teach So Students Remember

quote: Schools and districts have tools available to address one of their biggest challenges. But students have to actually use those tools to see any benefits.
author: Chad Aldeman
work: Ed Tech Can Be Good, Too

quote: We perform better on tests when in the same state of mind as when we studied.
author: Benedict Carey
work: How We Learn

quote: Students don't absorb new information in a vacuum -- they connect it to what they already know. A well-sequenced, knowledge-rich curriculum is essential.
author: Chad Aldeman
work: How Students Learn

quote: Some difficulties that elicit more effort and that slow down learning will more than compensate for their inconvenience by making the learning stronger, more precise, and more enduring.
author: Peter C. Brown, Henry L. Roediger III & Mark A. McDaniel
work: Make It Stick

quote: Project-based learning can be valuable when students already possess a strong foundation of knowledge and skills. Too often, however, schools treat projects as a substitute for content rather than an opportunity to apply content.
author: Chad Aldeman
work: In the Age of AI Knowledge Matters More Not Less

quote: Creative thinking lessons build on critical thinking and go beyond simple recall to consider "what if" possibilities and incorporate real-life problem solving.
author: Patti Drapeau
work: Sparking Student Creativity

quote: But as homework demands started to drop off, so did student achievement scores. While fewer students were being given homework, the scores for the lowest achievers fell particularly fast.
author: Aldeman, Chad
work: The Rise and Fall of Homework

quote: Human learning is a slow process that can happen over months and years rather than hours or days.
author: John Hattie
work: Visible Learning and the Science of How We Learn

quote: Subjective acceleration choices, like those depending strictly on teacher recommendation, leave too much room for bias and unchecked discretion.
author: Chad Aldeman
work: When Should You Accelerate Your Child?

quote: Surface learning is not shallow learning. It is not about rote skills and meaningless algorithms.
author: John Hattie
work: Visible Learning for Mathematics

quote: Engelmann is best understood as last, not first. His methods are invaluable in certain circumstances. But they only become applicable after a series of much harder decisions have already been made.
author: Becky Allen
work: Engelmann Last

quote: Any lesson planning must begin with a deep understanding of what each student already knows and can do, and how the instruction is aimed at increasing the progress and levels of achievement for each student.
author: John Hattie
work: Visible Learning for Teachers

quote: The trouble with instruction is that it is cheap to propose and expensive to verify.
author: Becky Allen
work: How Teaching Might Finally Learn

quote: The notion that increasing achievement is a function of our efforts and interest is critical to success — there is no point in investing in study or preparation if we do not believe that our efforts can make a difference.
author: John Hattie
work: Visible Learning: A Synthesis of Over 800 Meta-Analyses Relating to Achievement

quote: Höffler and Leutner found a large effect for spatial content and almost nothing for declarative content. 'Video is engaging' does not explain that pattern. An actual explanation would.
author: Becky Allen
work: Plausible Stories Are Not Explanations

quote: The more words we have, the more packets of knowledge, and, hence, the more background knowledge we have.
author: Robert J. Marzano
work: Building Background Knowledge for Academic Achievement

quote: For the first time, we have tools with the right architecture to do remediation the way tutors actually do it. Not by finding the right pre-built chunk, but by not chunking at all.
author: Becky Allen
work: Teaching Backwards From the Mind

quote: One of the greatest obstacles to learning is the fear of making mistakes. Because many students equate making mistakes with feeling humiliated, they will avoid learning tasks that appear too challenging.
author: Tomlinson & Sousa
work: Differentiation and the Brain

quote: The performance is visible. The underlying capacity may not be. Gilbert Ryle made a similar distinction between reciting multiplication facts and calculating them: the same answer can conceal different capacities.
author: Becky Allen
work: The Reality of Understanding

quote: Teachers need professional development because the job of teaching is so difficult, so complex, that one lifetime is not enough to master it.
author: Dylan Wiliam
work: Embedded Formative Assessment

quote: Students in 7th grade should not have to memorize the 50 states; they should already know them by then because they have had rich experiences learning about them in elementary school.
author: Lauren Brown
work: Should Students Memorize the 50 States

quote: Children — like all learners — try to make sense of what they are learning by fitting it into what they already know or believe.
author: Susan A. Ambrose
work: How Learning Works: Seven Research-Based Principles for Smart Teaching

quote: The most important sentence of her presentation was, 'We can't develop what we can't name.' So carefully defining the work we do is imperative.
author: Lauren Brown
work: Takeaways from ResearchED NYC

quote: When we sleep, the brain reviews the events and tasks of the day, storing them more securely than at the time we originally processed them.
author: David A. Sousa
work: How the Brain Learns

quote: I've often ruminated about what retrieval practice—that all-important tool in learning—looks like in history class. And one of the obvious answers is writing.
author: Lauren Brown
work: Why We All Need to Care About Handwriting

quote: Working memory is occupied by a combination of intrinsic and extraneous load. The sum of the two cannot exceed working memory capacity if learning is to take place.
author: John Sweller
work: Cognitive Load Theory in Action

quote: Every time I read current arguments about history and social studies instruction, I have the same reaction: we have been here before.
author: Douglas Carnine
work: History: Teach It and Learn from It

quote: It is common knowledge that "too much" emotion can prevent clear thinking; but too little emotion can be associated with low engagement.
author: Michael Baker, Jerry Andriessen & Sanna Järvelä
work: Affective Learning Together

quote: The period of preventable harm can be defined as the interval of time between when evidence was established and when evidence was uniformly applied by the profession.
author: Douglas Carnine
work: What Education Can Learn From Trusted Professions

quote: When students possess some relevant knowledge, it can lead both students and instructors to assume that students are better prepared than they truly are for a particular task or level of instruction.
author: Susan A. Ambrose et al.
work: How Learning Works

quote: Active construction in sandbox games like Roblox, and immersion in narrative-rich worlds like Genshin Impact, appears to work on the mind differently than passive scrolling.
author: Cheng, Yung-hsun
work: The Impact of Online Games on Creativity and the Role of Imagination

quote: When you try to remember a list of numbers by saying them aloud, you are using a verbal memory loop, a part of immediate memory that can hold information for only about 2 seconds.
author: David A. Sousa
work: How the Brain Learns Mathematics

quote: What is not plausible is to pin these dramatic UK, Anglosphere and global trends on a specific set of education policy decisions that only affected England.
author: Christodoulou, Daisy
work: Do Knowledge-Rich Curriculums Cause Mental Health Problems

quote: We often see what we expect to see; in this way, advance organizers are powerful instruments for focusing our students' attention.
author: Marilee Sprenger
work: How to Teach So Students Remember

quote: For very young children, there is a lot of evidence that learning relies on eye contact, shared attention, imitation, emotional connection, and interactive back-and-forth conversation.
author: Daisy Christodoulou
work: Motorways with No Speed Limits and Car-Free Town Centres

quote: Our assumptions about learning are suspect, if not wrong. Having something going on in the study environment, like music, is better than nothing.
author: Benedict Carey
work: How We Learn

quote: The Trivium is the study of grammar, logic, and rhetoric, which train mind and tongue in the critical thinking, clear communication, and eloquence necessary for a well-rounded life.
author: Classical Ed Review
work: The Trivium

quote: It's one thing to feel confident of your knowledge; it's something else to demonstrate mastery.
author: Peter C. Brown, Henry L. Roediger III & Mark A. McDaniel
work: Make It Stick

quote: Book-starved and book-rich ELA classrooms can both be found in American schools. Predictably, curriculum is an essential influence.
author: Curriculum Insight Project
work: Unpacking the RAND Survey on Books in English Language Arts Curriculum

quote: Students with a fixed mindset do not believe effort and engagement make a difference; students with a growth mindset believe their skills and abilities can be developed.
author: Patti Drapeau
work: Sparking Student Creativity

quote: The most powerful thing a school can give a child is an education. We have spent too long being distracted from it.
author: Stephanie Dehghani
work: The Role of Schools Was Never the Problem

quote: Unless the material is strongly meaningful, relevant and timely, it is subject to rapid and substantial forgetting.
author: John Hattie
work: Visible Learning and the Science of How We Learn

quote: Productive struggle is not a feature of difficulty. It is a feature of how difficulty is designed. The same objective challenge can be productive or demoralising depending entirely on the scaffolding around it.
author: Patrick Dempsey
work: If Productive Struggle Is the Goal, You're Doing it Wrong

quote: It is through this early consolidation that they can begin to retrieve information efficiently, so that they make room for more complex problem solving.
author: John Hattie
work: Visible Learning for Mathematics

quote: Modern education measures how well a student's brain has adapted to an environment specifically engineered against how human brains actually encode and retrieve information.
author: Patrick Dempsey
work: If You're Defending Education Against AI, You Don't Understand Learning

quote: The key issue is that children may think differently from adults, which means that attention needs to be given to how and not only to what the child is learning.
author: John Hattie
work: Visible Learning for Teachers

quote: Negative experiences are expensive. If a learning experience takes from students repeatedly without giving enough back, the relationship to you and the course cannot but degrade.
author: Dempsey, Patrick
work: Students Are Customers

quote: Achievement is more likely to be increased when students invoke learning rather than performance strategies, accept rather than discount feedback, and benchmark to difficult rather than to easy goals.
author: John Hattie
work: Visible Learning: A Synthesis of Over 800 Meta-Analyses Relating to Achievement

quote: If you want to test the effect of a true phone ban on learning, phones likely need to be genuinely removed from the school environment bell-to-bell.
author: The Digital Delusion
work: Researchers Say Phone Bans Dont Improve Learning But They Didnt Study Bans

quote: The research and theory strongly suggest that teaching vocabulary is synonymous with teaching background knowledge.
author: Robert J. Marzano
work: Building Background Knowledge for Academic Achievement

quote: I keep returning to chess because it illustrates something important about mathematics learning: expertise depends heavily on automaticity. What becomes automatic frees up mental space for what actually matters.
author: Edulore
work: Chess Masters and Maths Fluency

quote: Fear is an intense emotional response that shuts down higher cognitive processes so the brain can focus on the source of the fear and decide how to deal with it.
author: Tomlinson & Sousa
work: Differentiation and the Brain

quote: Pupils with higher prior attainment in maths who were taught in mixed-attainment maths classes made less progress than those taught in setted maths classes. (Equivalent to about two months' less progress.)
author: EEF Editorial Team
work: Evidence Edit Attainment Grouping and Independent Learning

quote: No teacher is so good—or so bad—that he or she cannot improve. That is why we need professional development.
author: Dylan Wiliam
work: Embedded Formative Assessment

quote: Children are not fragile information processors waiting to be protected from difficulty. They are unusually exploratory, socially attuned, and cognitively plastic.
author: Peter Ellerton
work: Beyond Cognitive Load Theory

quote: A single correction or refutation is unlikely to be enough to help students revise deeply held misconceptions; guiding students through conceptual change takes time, patience, and creativity.
author: Susan A. Ambrose
work: How Learning Works: Seven Research-Based Principles for Smart Teaching

quote: Intelligence is usually treated as a general cognitive capacity. People often think of it as something you're born with: the mental horsepower that allows you to process information quickly, recognise patterns or make connections.
author: Peter Ellerton
work: Critical Thinking, Expertise and Intelligence

quote: Merely expecting that a memory will be needed for a future test determined whether or not sleep significantly benefitted the consolidation of that memory.
author: David A. Sousa
work: How the Brain Learns

quote: When learning a new language, nothing can make you feel like you are making progress more rapidly than to attack something in a new language with which you are already familiar in your old one.
author: Andrew Ellison
work: Born Again, A Language Odyssey

quote: The primary difference between novices and experts in a given domain is that experts possess a greater amount of relevant domain-specific knowledge.
author: John Sweller
work: Cognitive Load Theory in Action

quote: THINK OF SOME WORD THAT STARTS WITH THE SAME FIRST LETTER THAT YOU SEE HERE. IT'S PROBABLY CLOSE ENOUGH.
author: Andrew Ellison
work: Search Your Feelings

quote: A positive mood has been shown to be associated with greater creativity and flexibility in problem-solving, as well as better decision-making.
author: Michael Baker, Jerry Andriessen & Sanna Järvelä

author: Nick Hart
work: Team Meetings: A Driver of School Improvement

quote: The stuff that gets termed 'implementation' is the actual work of reform. Legislative wins, no matter how tough, are just an opening act.
author: Frederick Hess
source: Education Next / Old School with Rick Hess
work: Five Lessons for School Reformers: 2026 Edition

quote: Because her brain wasn't stressed, she started remembering things she thought she'd forgotten. She relaxed. Because she had spent weeks solidifying her foundations, her comfort zone naturally began to expand.
author: (Keller, Shari)
work: The Simple Strategy That Turns Summer Boredom into Independent Projects

quote: Failure implies something is broken. Being undertraveled means something hasn't been built yet. And building is something we know how to do.
author: (Lippert, Samantha)
work: The Red Carpet Effect A New Way to See Reading Fluency

quote: The outcome is important, but you also want to figure out what proof of learning would reasonably look like. How would you know someone is actually absorbing and applying what you're teaching?
author: Melissa Muth Martinez
work: Start With The End

quote: The framework asks novices to do what experts do. But novices are not slow experts. They are people who think differently, because they know differently.
author: Olivia Mullins
work: NGSS: A Journey From Excitement to Disillusion

quote: We design plans based on how we hope the system behaves, not how it actually behaves.
author: Mr. Nickhart
work: Predictably Unpredictable

quote: What is more compelling than a narrative. Stories drive behaviour and are memorable; finding the right narrative for the school community to bring to life the improvement works at the heart of school leadership.
author: Mr. Nickhart
work: The Anatomy of Strategy

quote: The design move that makes the difference is the one most schools never make. The student culture system serves two humans, not one, and its job is to design experiences that reinforce each other rather than compete.
author: Chris O'Brien
work: Do It for the Kids and the Adults Too

quote: A leadership team should leave the summer able to answer one question instantly, in unison: when this system breaks in October, who handles it and how? If you get blank stares, you have more summer work to do.
author: Chris O'Brien
work: School Improvement 201: What to Actually Do With Your Summer

quote: Column two is not your job. Column two is a list of systems you haven't built yet. Every item in it is a coaching system, a decision rule, a defined role, or a piece of trained judgment waiting to exist.
author: Chris O'Brien
work: The Last System Youll Ever Build Is Yourself

quote: Employees who feel supported by their administration are 70% less likely to burn out, so if you are feeling unsupported, a new school with a new supervisor might be the answer.
author: Ruth Poulsen
work: Should I Stay or Should I Go

quote: What matters is that you don't stay stuck in the fog out of guilt, or a belief that enduring harm is somehow part of the job. It isn't. Our schools shouldn't be chewing people up and spitting them out.
author: Regenerative Schools
work: Should I Stay or Should I Go Three Questions to Cut Through the Fog of Burnout

quote: 'Show me your best thinking.' / 'I know you don't know the answer. Show me your best thinking. Then I can work out how to help you get even better.'
author: Dominic Salles
work: Get them to behave with no skills whatsoever Part 3 The Language You Use

quote: The Agreeable Taker is the most dangerous hire you will ever make. They seem warm, they seem collaborative, and they are quietly extracting value from everyone around them.
author: Dominic Salles
work: How to Create a School of Givers Not Takers

quote: We focused on them because they were 'proxies for learning'. Not teaching. Teaching is simply a proxy for learning. Learning is the end goal.
author: Salles, Dominic
work: Quality Assurance that Works

quote: The national results are shocking and should concern every school leader.
author: Dominic Salles
work: Student Voice Is Shocking

quote: Learning sticks when it arrives as an answer to a question the learner is already asking. Don't start with the rule. Start with something concrete and well-chosen that makes the learner lean in and creates a need to know in them.
author: (Santana, Javier)
work: Inductive and Deductive Learning The Power of Guided Discovery

quote: The science of reading is an overnight success that was 30 years in the making... when it comes to math, the learning science says we need to learn a few dances, not just one.
author: Randy Squier & Mike Petrilli
work: Portrait Is Not a Substitute for Rigor

quote: Benchmark Assessment System is still on approved curriculum lists because approval processes are not designed to filter out ineffective programmes. They are designed to provide legal cover.
author: Karen Vaites
work: The New Fordham Report Is Exhibit 729 for Policy Is Not Progress

quote: Teachers made it clear that testing was the major influence shaping their instruction: 'The most important thing is whatever test we're giving, because it is how I'm judged as a teacher.'
author: Natalie Wexler
work: Is Testing the Next Frontier in Ed Reform Louisiana Experiment

quote: Students learn math because their expedition requires calculations, not because they're told math is important.
author: Patrick Whalen
work: Expeditionary Education

quote: When effort doesn't produce visible progress, effort becomes punishment.
author: Patrick Whalen
work: The Pleasure of Effort

quote: Including people in what they are thinking, contemplating, seeing, and concluding is what good leaders do to bring people along. It is well worth the effort. Without it, teams flounder with uncertainty and weak execution.
author: Admired Leadership Field Notes

quote: Most teacher learning is informal, happening as we go about our day to day work. Not in formal courses or twilight INSET sessions but in conversation with each other. So, we're prioritising collaboration but by what means?
author: Nick Hart
work: Not Everyone Needs an Action Plan

quote: The ladder does something dangerous to our imagination. It makes movement feel like failure unless the movement is upward.
author: Jolein
work: Burn the Ladder
